text:
**The fix:** `who --all-projects` fell back to the old `WhoSnapshot`/`render_who_*` markdown-table renderer because the daemon's `rpc_who` only builds the unified fabric view when a single project scope resolves — `--all-projects` has no single scope, so that branch was skipped entirely (this was a known, documented open tail from the 2026-06-29 session that unified `who` with hook injection). I added `render_fabric_all_projects`/`_human` in `fabric_context.rs` (one project block per root channel, roster rendered once up front) and wired it into the `all_projects` branch of `rpc_who`. Compiled clean, clippy clean, all existing `who`/`fabric_context` unit tests pass, and a new isolated integration test confirms the unified format renders (still finishing a wrinkle in that test around session-id anchoring for the second project).

**Your new question — any other stray renderers duplicating this work?** Checked: `turn_context/start.rs` (turn-start hook injection) and `turn_context/check.rs` (PostToolUse) both already call the *same* `render_fabric_context` I just reused — so hook injection, single-project `who`, and now `--all-projects` all go through one pipeline (`build_view` → `render_view`/`render_human_view`). The only other renderer left is the old `src/cli/who/render.rs` + `src/who_snapshot.rs` table — but it's now purely a fallback for when the daemon doesn't emit a `fabric`/`fabric_human` string at all (e.g. version-skewed daemon), on both code paths, not an active duplicate. No other stray renderers found (statusline, debug TUI, chat-read-row are legitimately different surfaces, not copies of this one).
